status_notes: Initial sweep entry — not deeply researched, added to capture the source for later processing.
notes: See also registry/entity/gov/cisa.json (the agency itself) and registry/disclosure/gov/cisa.json (CVE CNA program).

match_nodes.description: CISA Secure by Design principles and pledge program

data.notes: Voluntary, non-binding initiative with two parts: a set of principles urging software manufacturers to build security in at the design phase rather than bolt it on, and a companion Pledge structured around concrete one-year goals (e.g. MFA by default, reducing default passwords, reducing common vulnerability classes like path traversal/SQLi/XSS, CVE quality, vulnerability disclosure policies). 68+ manufacturers had signed as of the initial 2024 announcement.

match_nodes.description: CISA Binding Operational Directives (BODs) and Emergency Directives (EDs)

data.notes: BODs are compulsory, statute-backed directives to federal civilian executive branch agencies establishing ongoing security requirements (e.g. vulnerability remediation timelines, asset inventory); EDs are used for rapid, short-fuse mitigation of an actively-exploited critical threat. This is a landing-page-level citation, not a specific directive — individual BODs/EDs are numbered and superseded frequently (e.g. BOD 26-04 on risk-based vulnerability remediation consolidated earlier directives in early 2026).
